title: Learning to Learn
objective: Students will explore learning to learn concepts including Asking for Help, Persisting When It's Hard, Checking Your Own Work.

steps.content: Let's discover the amazing world of Asking for Help!
Ask for help when you've had a go yourself and are still stuck — knowing when to ask is a skill in itself
Time to try this out: Hattie visible learning
If you has tried something and is still stuck, do they come and ask for help rather than sitting frustrated or giving up?
Great job! Today we learned about Asking for Help. Hattie visible learning self-regulated learning research
Today we're going to learn about Persisting When It's Hard. Get ready for an adventure!
Keep trying when something feels hard — making mistakes and trying again is how learning happens
Let me show you something cool. Keep working on a difficult task for a sustained period without giving up or asking someone else to do it
If you finds a puzzle or activity really tricky, do they keep trying different ways rather than giving up straight away?

Great job! Today we learned about Persisting When It's Hard. Keep working on a difficult task for a sustained period without giving up or asking someone else to do it After making a mistake, try again with a different approach rather than saying 'I can't do it'
Have you ever thought about Checking Your Own Work? Let's find out together!
After finishing a task, look back at what you did and ask yourself: does this seem right?
Let me show you something cool. After finishing a piece of work, re-read or review it without being asked to
After you finishes a piece of work or activity, do they look it over themselves to check it seems right before saying they're done?

Great job! Today we learned about Checking Your Own Work. After finishing a piece of work, re-read or review it without being asked to Spot and correct at least one error in their own work by checking back through it
